input.content:
---
name: zsh-completion
description: >
  Write Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework. Use this skill whenever asked to
  create, write, fix, debug, or improve a Zsh completion function (_arguments, compdef, #compdef,
  tab completion). Also trigger when seeing zsh autocompletion, shell completion scripts, _compdef tags,
  or requests involving command-line tab completion for any CLI tool. Even if the user just says
  "add completions for X" or "make tab completion work", this skill applies.
---

Write a Zsh completion script for the specified command. $ARGUMENTS

# Zsh Completion Skill

This skill produces high-quality Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework.
Never use the legacy `compctl` system.

## Research Phase

Before writing any completion code, understand the command's full interface:

1. Read `--help` output, man page, or source code
2. Identify the option style: GNU long opts, POSIX short, subcommands, flags-only, mixed
3. Map out subcommand trees if they exist
4. Note which options take arguments and what types (files, dirs, choices, etc.)
5. Check for dynamic completions (e.g., `docker ps` for container names)

For authoritative reference on compsys internals, read:
- Builtin commands (compadd, compset): `references/compsys-builtins.md`
- Utility functions (_arguments, _describe, etc.): `references/utility-functions.md`
- Zsh HTML docs: `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/htmldoc/Completion-System.html`
- Real-world examples: `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/` (look at `_git`, `_docker`, `_ssh`)

## Structure Decision

Choose the right structure based on command complexity:

| Command Style | Structure | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Simple flags only | Flat `_arguments` | `_ls` |
| Flags + subcommands | `_arguments -C` + state machine | `_git` |
| Keyword/value lists | `_values` | feature flags |
| Multiple tag sources | `_alternative` | user@host |
| Regex-based grammar | `_regex_arguments` | complex DSLs |

## Standard Template

```zsh
#compdef mycmd

_mycmd() {
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1
  typeset -A opt_args

  _arguments -C -S -s \
    '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
    '(- :)'{-V,--version}'[show version]' \
    '(-v --verbose)'{-v,--verbose}'[verbose output]' \
    '(-o --output)'{-o,--output}'[output file]:file:_files' \
    '1:command:->commands' \
    '*::arg:->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    commands)
      local -a commands=(
        'init:initialize project'
        'build:build project'
        'test:run tests'
      )
      _describe 'command' commands && ret=0
      ;;
    args)
      curcontext="${curcontext%:*:*}:mycmd-$line[1]:"
      case $line[1] in
        init)  _mycmd_init ;;
        build) _mycmd_build ;;
        test)  _mycmd_test ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac
  return ret
}

_mycmd "$@"
```

## _arguments Quick Reference

### Flags to _arguments

| Flag | Effect |
|------|--------|
| `-s` | Enable single-letter option stacking: `-abc` = `-a -b -c` |
| `-S` | Stop completing options after `--` |
| `-C` | Modify `curcontext` for `->state` actions |
| `-w` | With `-s`, allow stacking even when options take arguments |
| `-W` | Extends `-w`: complete options after inline args |
| `-A pat` | No options after first non-option matching `pat` (e.g. `-A "-*"`) |
| `-n` | Set `NORMARG` to position of first normal argument |
| `-0` | Use NUL separator in `opt_args` values instead of colon |

### Option Spec Forms

```
-x          arg in next word
-x-         arg must be in same word: -xARG
-x+         arg in same word or next: -xARG or -x ARG
-x=         arg after = or next word: -x=ARG or -x ARG
-x=-        arg must follow =: -x=ARG only
*-x         option may be repeated
-+x / +-x   both -x and +x are valid
```

### Exclusion Lists (parenthesized prefix)

```zsh
'(-b -c)-a[desc]'                          # -a excludes -b, -c
'(-v --verbose)'{-v,--verbose}'[verbose]'  # short/long mutual (brace expansion)
'(- :)'{-h,--help}'[help]'                # - = no options, : = no args, * = no rest-args
'(group2)-a[desc]'                        # exclude entire option group
```

### Argument Specs

```
n:message:action       nth positional (required)
n::message:action      nth positional (optional)
:message:action        next positional (required)
::message:action       next positional (optional)
*:message:action       all remaining
*::message:action      remaining (words/CURRENT = non-option args only)
*:::message:action     remaining (words/CURRENT = this spec's args only)
```

### Actions

| Action | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `(item1 item2)` | Complete from list |
| `((item1\:desc1 item2\:desc2))` | List with descriptions |
| `->state` | Set `$state`, handle in case block |
| `_files` | All files |
| `_files -/` | Directories only |
| `_files -g '*.go'` | Files matching glob |
| `{eval-string}` | Eval shell code to generate matches |
| ` ` (space) | Message only, no completions |
| `func arg...` | Call function (compadd opts auto-inserted) |
| `=action` | Insert dummy word, then do action (for nested _arguments) |

### Option Groups (mutually exclusive)

```zsh
_arguments \
  + '(operation)' \
    {-c,--compress}'[compress]' \
    {-d,--decompress}'[decompress]'
```

Options in `(name)` group are mutually exclusive. Access: `$opt_args[operation--c]`.

### Argument Sets (mutually exclusive modes)

```zsh
_arguments \
  -a \
  - set1 -c ':arg:(x y)' \
  - set2 -d ':arg:(a b)'
```

### Auto-generate from --help

```zsh
_arguments -- \
  '*=FILE*:file:_files' \
  '*=DIR*:directory:_files -/' \
  '*\*:toggle:(yes no)'
```

## Key Utility Functions

### _describe -- items with descriptions
```zsh
local -a items=('opt1:description one' 'opt2:description two')
_describe 'thing' items
```

### _alternative -- multiple tag sources
```zsh
_alternative \
  'users:user:_users' \
  'hosts:host:_hosts' \
  'files:file:_files'
```

### _values -- keyword/flag completion (optionally separated)
```zsh
_values -s , 'feature' \
  'debug[enable debug]' \
  'verbose[verbose output]' \
  'color[colors]::when:(auto always never)'
```

### _files -- file/directory completion
```zsh
_files                        # all files
_files -/                     # directories only
_files -g '*.{js,ts}'         # glob pattern
_files -W $dir                # relative to $dir
```

### _pick_variant -- handle command variants (GNU vs BSD)
```zsh
local variant
_pick_variant -r variant gnu='(GNU|uutils)' bsd unix --version
case $variant in
  gnu) args+=('(-v --verbose)'{-v,--verbose}'[verbose]') ;;
  *)   args=( ${${${args:#(|*\))--*}//--[^ )]#/}/\( #\)/} ) ;;  # strip long opts
esac
```

### _sequence -- separated lists
```zsh
_sequence -s : _files -/   # colon-separated dirs (like PATH)
```

### _multi_parts -- path-like segment completion
```zsh
local -a pkgs=(com.example.foo com.example.bar)
_multi_parts . pkgs   # completes each dot-segment individually
```

### _sep_parts -- multi-segment with separator
```zsh
_sep_parts '(alice bob)' @ '(host1 host2)'
```

### _guard -- conditional display
```zsh
_arguments '-n-: :_guard "[0-9]#" "numeric value"'
```

### _message -- help text without matches
```zsh
_message 'enter a URL'
```

### _call_program -- run external command (user-overridable via zstyle)
```zsh
local -a output=( ${(f)"$(_call_program targets make -qp 2>/dev/null)"} )
```

### _gnu_generic -- quick completion from --help
```zsh
compdef _gnu_generic mycommand
```

### Caching
```zsh
local cache_id="mycmd_completions"
if _cache_invalid $cache_id || ! _retrieve_cache $cache_id; then
  local -a completions=( $(expensive_command) )
  _store_cache $cache_id completions
fi
compadd -a completions
```

## Common Patterns

### Subcommand Dispatch
```zsh
_mycmd() {
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1
  typeset -A opt_args

  _arguments -C \
    '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[help]' \
    '(-v --verbose)'{-v,--verbose}'[verbose]' \
    '1:command:->commands' \
    '*::arg:->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    commands)
      local -a commands=(
        'start:start service'
        'stop:stop service'
        'status:show status'
      )
      _describe 'command' commands && ret=0
      ;;
    args)
      curcontext="${curcontext%:*:*}:mycmd-$line[1]:"
      case $line[1] in
        start)
          _arguments \
            '(-d --daemon)'{-d,--daemon}'[daemonize]' \
            '(-p --port)'{-p,--port}'[port]:port:' && ret=0 ;;
        stop)
          _arguments '(-f --force)'{-f,--force}'[force]' && ret=0 ;;
        status)
          _arguments '(-j --json)'{-j,--json}'[JSON output]' && ret=0 ;;
      esac ;;
  esac
  return ret
}
```

### Dynamic Completion from Command Output
```zsh
_mycmd_targets() {
  local -a targets
  targets=( ${(f)"$(mycmd list-targets 2>/dev/null)"} )
  _describe 'target' targets
}
```

### Completion from Config File
```zsh
_mycmd_profiles() {
  local config="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/mycmd/config.yaml"
  [[ -f $config ]] || return 1
  local -a profiles=( ${(f)"$(grep '^[a-zA-Z]' $config | sed 's/:.*//')"} )
  _describe 'profile' profiles
}
```

### Brace Expansion for Short/Long Options
```zsh
'(-q --quiet -v --verbose)'{-q,--quiet}'[quiet]'
'(-q --quiet -v --verbose)'{-v,--verbose}'[verbose]'
```

### Repeatable Options
```zsh
'*'{-I,--include}'[include path]:dir:_files -/'
'*'{-D,--define}'[define]:macro:'
```

### Inline Value (no space)
```zsh
'-O-[optimization]:level:(0 1 2 3 s z)'    # -O2
'-O+[optimization]:level:(0 1 2 3 s z)'    # -O2 or -O 2
```

### Nested Subcommands
```zsh
# Inside args handler:
case $line[1] in
  remote)
    _arguments -C \
      '1:remote cmd:->remote_cmds' \
      '*::remote arg:->remote_args' && ret=0
    case $state in
      remote_cmds)
        _describe 'remote command' '(add:add remove:remove list:list)' && ret=0 ;;
      remote_args)
        case $line[1] in
          add) _arguments ':name:' ':url:' && ret=0 ;;
        esac ;;
    esac ;;
esac
```

## Low-Level: compadd

```zsh
compadd -X 'header' -d descs -a matches
compadd -P 'prefix' -S 'suffix' -q -- items
compadd -f -W $dir -- $files
```

Key flags: `-P`/`-S` prefix/suffix, `-d` display array, `-X` explanation, `-f` filenames, `-q` auto-remove suffix, `-J` sorted group, `-V` unsorted group, `-M` matchspec, `-F` filter, `-W` dir prefix, `-U` use regardless of word, `-l` one per line, `-o` ordering, `-E` empty matches for layout, `-n` add but don't list.

For the full compadd/compset reference, read `references/compsys-builtins.md`.

## compset -- Modify PREFIX/SUFFIX

```zsh
compset -P '*='      # strip prefix through '='
compset -P 'prefix'  # strip literal prefix
compset -S ':*'      # strip suffix from ':'
compset -q           # handle quoted words
compset -n 2         # remove first word from $words, adjust $CURRENT
compset -N '|'       # narrow words to after last '|'
```

## Special Parameters

- `$words` -- array of command line words
- `$CURRENT` -- index of current word
- `$PREFIX` / `$SUFFIX` -- text before/after cursor in current word
- `$IPREFIX` / `$ISUFFIX` -- ignored prefix/suffix (not part of match)
- `$compstate[context]` -- completion context type
- `$compstate[insert]` -- insertion mode
- `$compstate[nmatches]` -- matches added so far

## Testing & Debugging

```zsh
unfunction _mycmd; autoload -U _mycmd   # reload
# Ctrl-x h  -- show contexts and tags
# Ctrl-x ?  -- trace completion execution
source ./_mycmd                         # source directly
```

## Installation

File must be named `_commandname`, start with `#compdef commandname`, and live in `$fpath`:

```zsh
# In .zshrc before compinit:
fpath=(/path/to/completions $fpath)
autoload -U compinit && compinit
```

## Critical Rules

1. First line: `#compdef cmdname`
2. File name: `_cmdname` (underscore prefix)
3. Escape literal colons in specs: `\:`
4. Use brace expansion for short/long pairs: `'(-v --verbose)'{-v,--verbose}'[desc]'`
5. Declare `local curcontext state line ret=1` and `typeset -A opt_args` when using `->state`
6. Return `ret` (0 = matches added)
7. Prefer `_files` over `_path_files`
8. Use `_call_program` for external commands (user-overridable)
9. Wrap: `_mycmd() { ... }; _mycmd "$@"` for proper scoping
10. Test with actual Tab completion, not just syntax

---
name: zsh-completion
description: >
  Write Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework. Use this skill whenever asked to
  create, write, fix, debug, or improve a Zsh completion function (_arguments, compdef, #compdef,
  tab completion). Also trigger when seeing zsh autocompletion, shell completion scripts, _compdef tags,
  or requests involving command-line tab completion for any CLI tool. Even if the user just says
  "add completions for X" or "make tab completion work", this skill applies.
---

Write a Zsh completion script for the specified command. $ARGUMENTS

# Zsh Completion Skill

This skill produces high-quality Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework.
Never use the legacy `compctl` system.

## Research Phase

Before writing any completion code, understand the command's full interface:

1. Read `--help` output, man page, or source code
2. Identify the option style: GNU long opts, POSIX short, subcommands, flags-only, mixed
3. Map out subcommand trees if they exist
4. Note which options take arguments and what types (files, dirs, choices, etc.)
5. Check for dynamic completions (e.g., `docker ps` for container names)

For authoritative reference on compsys internals, read:
- Builtin commands (compadd, compset): `references/compsys-builtins.md`
- Utility functions (_arguments, _describe, etc.): `references/utility-functions.md`
- Zsh HTML docs: `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/htmldoc/Completion-System.html`
- Real-world examples: `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/functions/` (look at `_git`, `_docker`, `_ssh`)

## Structure Decision

Choose the right structure based on command complexity:

| Command Style | Structure | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Simple flags only | Flat `_arguments` | `_ls` |
| Flags + subcommands | `_arguments -C` + state machine | `_git` |
| Keyword/value lists | `_values` | feature flags |

        init)  _mycmd_init ;;

  esac
  return ret
}

# Compsys Builtin Commands Reference

This file covers the low-level completion builtins: `compadd`, `compset`, condition codes,
and match specifications. These are documented in Zsh's Chapter 19 (Completion Widgets).

For the HTML source: `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/htmldoc/Completion-Widgets.html`

## compadd

The core builtin for adding completion matches. Returns 0 if at least one match was added.

The completion code breaks each match into seven fields:
`<ipre><apre><hpre><body><hsuf><asuf><isuf>`

### Flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-P prefix` | String inserted before match (not part of match, not quoted) |
| `-S suffix` | String inserted after match (not part of match, not quoted) |
| `-p hidden-prefix` | Part of match but hidden in listings |
| `-s hidden-suffix` | Part of match but hidden in listings |
| `-i ignored-prefix` | Inserted before `-P`, from IPREFIX |
| `-I ignored-suffix` | Like `-i` but for suffix |
| `-a` | Treat completions as array names, use their values |
| `-k` | Treat completions as assoc array names, use their keys |
| `-d array` | Per-completion display strings |
| `-l` | With `-d`, list one per line instead of columns |
| `-o [order]` | Sort order: `match`, `nosort`, `numeric`, `reverse` |
| `-J group` | Sorted group name |
| `-V group` | Unsorted group name (same as `-J` + `-o nosort`) |
| `-1` | With `-V`, remove only consecutive duplicates |
| `-2` | With `-J`/`-V`, keep all duplicates |
| `-X explanation` | Header text above group (supports `%B`, `%S`, `%U`, `%F`, `%K`) |
| `-x message` | Like `-X` but shown even with no matches |
| `-q` | Auto-remove `-S` suffix on blank/same-char |
| `-r remove-chars` | Remove `-S` suffix when next typed char is in class |
| `-R remove-func` | Call function to decide suffix removal |
| `-f` | Mark matches as filenames (enables LIST_TYPES indicators, auto `/`) |
| `-e` | Mark matches as parameter names (enables AUTO_PARAM_SLASH/KEYS) |
| `-W file-prefix` | Prepend to match for file testing (with `-f`) |
| `-F array` | Patterns of completions to ignore |
| `-Q` | Don't quote metacharacters when inserting |
| `-M match-spec` | Local match specification (may be repeated) |
| `-n` | Add matches but don't list them |
| `-U` | Add all completions unconditionally (no matching) |
| `-O array` | Store matching completions in array instead of adding |
| `-A array` | Like `-O` but stores internally-generated strings |
| `-D array` | Remove non-matching elements from array |
| `-C` | Add special "all matches" completion |
| `-E number` | Add empty matches for layout (implies `-V -2`) |
| `--` | End of flags |

### Examples

```zsh
# Basic completion with display strings
local -a matches=(foo bar baz)
local -a descs=('foo - first option' 'bar - second option' 'baz - third option')
compadd -d descs -a matches

# With prefix and auto-removable suffix
compadd -P 'http://' -S '/' -q -- example.com github.com

# File completions relative to directory
compadd -f -W /usr/local/bin -- $files

# Explanation header
compadd -X 'Available targets:' -- clean build test

# Unsorted group with ordering preserved
compadd -V 'recent' -o nosort -- $recent_items
```

## compset

Modifies the special parameters (PREFIX, SUFFIX, IPREFIX, ISUFFIX, words, CURRENT).
Returns 0 if the test succeeds and parameters were modified.

### Forms

| Form | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `compset -p number` | Move first N chars from PREFIX to IPREFIX |
| `compset -P [n] pattern` | Move PREFIX matching pattern to IPREFIX (longest match; `n` for nth match) |
| `compset -s number` | Move last N chars from SUFFIX to ISUFFIX |
| `compset -S [n] pattern` | Move SUFFIX matching pattern to ISUFFIX |
| `compset -n begin [end]` | Remove words before `begin` (and after `end`) from `$words`, adjust `$CURRENT` |
| `compset -N beg-pat [end-pat]` | Like `-n` but matches patterns in `$words` |
| `compset -q` | Split current word on spaces (respecting quotes) into `$words` |

### Examples

```zsh
# Strip everything up to and including '=' from prefix
if compset -P '*\='; then
  # Now complete the value after '='
  _files
fi

# Strip the first match of '=' (not the longest)
compset -P 1 '*\='

# Handle key=value pairs
if compset -P '*\='; then
  _values_for_key "${IPREFIX%=}"
else
  compadd -S '=' -- ${keys}
fi

# Narrow words array to after a subcommand
compset -N '|'  # narrow to after last pipe
compset -n 2    # remove first word, adjust CURRENT
```

## Completion Condition Codes

For use inside `[[ ... ]]`. These test the same conditions as compset but
do NOT modify the special parameters.

| Condition | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `-prefix [n] pattern` | True if `-P` compset test would succeed |
| `-suffix [n] pattern` | True if `-S` compset test would succeed |
| `-after beg-pat` | True if `-N` test (beg only) would succeed |
| `-between beg-pat end-pat` | True if `-N` test (both pats) would succeed |

```zsh
if [[ -prefix *: ]]; then
  # We're after a colon, but don't modify PREFIX
  ...
fi
```

## Completion Special Parameters

These are special inside completion widgets and functions called from them.

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `$CURRENT` | Index of current word in `$words` |
| `$PREFIX` | Text from word start to cursor |
| `$SUFFIX` | Text from cursor to word end |
| `$IPREFIX` | Ignored prefix (not part of matching) |
| `$ISUFFIX` | Ignored suffix |
| `$QIPREFIX` | Quoted string prefix (read-only) |
| `$QISUFFIX` | Quoted string suffix (read-only) |
| `$words` | Array of command line words |

### compstate keys

| Key | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| `context` | Overall context: `command`, `condition`, `math`, `redirect`, `value`, etc. |
| `exact` | Set to `accept` if exact match should be accepted |
| `insert` | Controls insertion: `unambiguous`, `menu`, `automenu`, number, `all` |
| `list` | Controls listing: `list`, `autolist`, `ambiguous`; add `force`, `packed`, `rows` |
| `nmatches` | Number of matches added so far |
| `old_list` | `yes` if valid list from previous completion; set to `keep` to reuse |
| `parameter` | Parameter name when completing subscript/value |
| `pattern_match` | `*` if GLOB_COMPLETE set; controls pattern matching |
| `quote` | Quotation character when inside quotes |
| `quoting` | `single`, `double`, or `backtick` when inside quotes |
| `redirect` | Redirection operator when in redirect position |
| `restore` | `auto` by default; unset to prevent parameter restoration |
| `to_end` | When cursor moves to end: `single`, `match`, `always` |
| `unambiguous` | Common prefix of all matches (read-only) |

## Completion Matching Control

Match specifications broaden the default pattern used to filter completions.
Passed via `compadd -M` or the `matcher-list`/`matcher` styles.

Each matcher has the form: `LETTER:word-pat=match-pat`

### Matcher Types

| Matcher | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `m:wpat=mpat` | For each substring matching wpat, also match mpat |
| `M:wpat=mpat` | Like `m:` but replaces match substring with word's |
| `b:wpat=mpat` | At beginning of word only |
| `B:wpat=mpat` | Like `b:` with replacement |
| `e:wpat=mpat` | At end of word only |
| `E:wpat=mpat` | Like `e:` with replacement |
| `l:\|wpat=mpat` | At left edge |
| `l:anchor\|wpat=mpat` | Left of anchor |
| `r:wpat\|=mpat` | At right edge |
| `r:wpat\|anchor=mpat` | Right of anchor |
| `x:` | Stop processing (override point) |

### Common Match Specs

```zsh
# Case-insensitive completion
m:{[:lower:]}={[:upper:]}

# Case-insensitive both ways  
m:{[:lower:][:upper:]}={[:upper:][:lower:]}

# Partial word matching on - and _
r:|[_-]=* r:|=*

# Substring matching (complete anywhere in string)
l:|=* r:|=*

# Complete dot-separated segments
r:|.=*

# CamelCase completion: fB -> fooBar
r:?||[[:upper:]]=*
```

Uppercase matchers (M, B, E, L, R) replace the matched portion with the word's
text. Lowercase matchers take precedence when both match.

Patterns support `?`, `[...]` bracket expressions, and `{...}` brace expressions.
Brace expressions map elements positionally (unlike brackets which match any).

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- use-modern-go: Apply modern Go syntax guidelines based on project's Go version. Use when user ask for modern Go code guidelines.
- zsh-completion: Write Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework. Use this skill whenever asked to create, write, fix, debug, or improve a Zsh completion function (_arguments, compdef, #compdef, tab completion). Also trigger when seeing zsh autocompletion, shell completion scripts, _compdef tags, or requests involving command-line tab completion for any CLI tool. Even if the user just says "add completions for X" or "make tab completion work", this skill applies.
- execplan: Create and execute structured ExecPlans — self-contained design documents that guide coding agents through complex implementations. Use this skill whenever the user's task involves multi-file changes, significant refactors, new features with multiple components, architectural changes, or any work that would benefit from a structured plan-then-execute approach. Also trigger when the user explicitly asks for an "execplan", "execution plan", or "structured plan". Even if the user doesn't mention planning, use this skill when the task is clearly too complex for ad-hoc implementation — e.g., tasks touching 3+ files, requiring research before coding, involving migrations, or needing coordinated changes across multiple packages or services. When in doubt about whether a task needs an ExecPlan, it probably does.
- zsh-completion: Write Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework. Use this skill whenever asked to create, write, fix, debug, or improve a Zsh completion function (_arguments, compdef, #compdef, tab completion). Also trigger when seeing zsh autocompletion, shell completion scripts, or _compdef tags.
- list-plan: List planned tasks
- commit-benchmark: git commit in multiple with Go benchmark results
- init-ultrathink: Generate AGENTS.md file ultrathink
- commit: git commit in multiple
- perf-plan: Investigate major performance improvements using the $LSP MCP server
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-reference: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills.
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-setup: Install or refresh oh-my-claudecode for plugin, npm, and local-dev setups from the canonical setup flow
- oh-my-claudecode:mcp-setup: Configure popular MCP servers for enhanced agent capabilities
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-doctor: Diagnose and fix oh-my-claudecode installation issues
- oh-my-claudecode:self-improve: Autonomous evolutionary code improvement engine with tournament selection
- oh-my-claudecode:ccg: Claude-Codex-Gemini tri-model orchestration via /ask codex + /ask gemini, then Claude synthesizes results
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-teams: CLI-team runtime for claude, codex, or gemini workers in tmux panes when you need process-based parallel execution
- oh-my-claudecode:project-session-manager: Worktree-first dev environment manager for issues, PRs, and features with optional tmux sessions
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-dive: 2-stage pipeline: trace (causal investigation) -> deep-interview (requirements crystallization) with 3-point injection
- oh-my-claudecode:hud: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements)
- oh-my-claudecode:cancel: Cancel any active OMC mode (autopilot, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, swarm, ultrapilot, pipeline, team)
- oh-my-claudecode:autopilot: Full autonomous execution from idea to working code
- oh-my-claudecode:ultrawork: Parallel execution engine for high-throughput task completion
- oh-my-claudecode:ultraqa: QA cycling workflow - test, verify, fix, repeat until goal met
- oh-my-claudecode:debug: Diagnose the current OMC session or repo state using logs, traces, state, and focused reproduction
- oh-my-claudecode:skillify: Turn a repeatable workflow from the current session into a reusable OMC skill draft
- oh-my-claudecode:trace: Evidence-driven tracing lane that orchestrates competing tracer hypotheses in Claude built-in team mode
- oh-my-claudecode:setup: Use first for install/update routing — sends setup, doctor, or MCP requests to the correct OMC setup flow
- oh-my-claudecode:plan: Strategic planning with optional interview workflow
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-interview: Socratic deep interview with mathematical ambiguity gating before autonomous execution
- oh-my-claudecode:ralplan: Consensus planning entrypoint that auto-gates vague ralph/autopilot/team requests before execution
- oh-my-claudecode:writer-memory: Agentic memory system for writers - track characters, relationships, scenes, and themes
- oh-my-claudecode:team: N coordinated agents on shared task list using Claude Code native teams
- oh-my-claudecode:deepinit: Deep codebase initialization with hierarchical AGENTS.md documentation
- oh-my-claudecode:sciomc: Orchestrate parallel scientist agents for comprehensive analysis with AUTO mode
- oh-my-claudecode:skill: Manage local skills - list, add, remove, search, edit, setup wizard
- oh-my-claudecode:external-context: Invoke parallel document-specialist agents for external web searches and documentation lookup
- oh-my-claudecode:remember: Review reusable project knowledge and decide what belongs in project memory, notepad, or durable docs
- oh-my-claudecode:ask: Process-first advisor routing for Claude, Codex, or Gemini via `omc ask`, with artifact capture and no raw CLI assembly
- oh-my-claudecode:wiki: LLM Wiki — persistent markdown knowledge base that compounds across sessions (Karpathy model)
- oh-my-claudecode:visual-verdict: Structured visual QA verdict for screenshot-to-reference comparisons
- oh-my-claudecode:learner: Extract a learned skill from the current conversation
- oh-my-claudecode:configure-notifications: Configure notification integrations (Telegram, Discord, Slack) via natural language
- oh-my-claudecode:verify: Verify that a change really works before you claim completion
- oh-my-claudecode:ralph: Self-referential loop until task completion with configurable verification reviewer
- oh-my-claudecode:release: Generic release assistant — analyzes repo release rules, caches them in .omc/RELEASE_RULE.md, then guides the release
- oh-my-claudecode:ai-slop-cleaner: Clean AI-generated code slop with a regression-safe, deletion-first workflow and optional reviewer-only mode
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- init: Initialize new CLAUDE.md file(s) and optional skills/hooks with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.content:
# Compsys Utility Functions Reference

This file covers the high-level utility functions from Chapter 20.6 of the Zsh manual.
These are the functions you'll use most when writing completion scripts.

For the HTML source: `/opt/homebrew/share/zsh/htmldoc/Completion-System.html`

## _arguments (full reference)

The primary function for command completion. Parses option and argument specifications.

### Signature

```
_arguments [-nswWCRS] [-A pat] [-O name] [-M matchspec] [:] spec ...
_arguments [opt...] -- [-l] [-i pats] [-s pair] [helpspec ...]
```

### Options to _arguments

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `-s` | Enable single-letter option stacking (`-abc` = `-a -b -c`) |
| `-S` | Stop completing options after `--`, ignore the `--` |
| `-w` | With `-s`, allow stacking even when options take arguments |
| `-W` | Extends `-w`: complete options after inline args |
| `-C` | Modify `curcontext` for `->state` actions |
| `-R` | Return 300 instead of 0 when `$state` is set |
| `-A pat` | No options after first non-option matching `pat` |
| `-O name` | Pass array elements as args to action functions |
| `-M matchspec` | Match spec for options/values (default: `r:|[_-]=* r:|=*`) |
| `-n` | Set `NORMARG` to position of first normal argument |
| `-0` | Use NUL separator in `opt_args` instead of colon |

### Spec Forms

**Positional arguments:**
```
n:message:action       # nth positional (required)
n::message:action      # nth positional (optional)
:message:action        # next positional (required)
::message:action       # next positional (optional)
*:message:action       # all remaining
*::message:action      # remaining (words/CURRENT = non-option args only)
*:::message:action     # remaining (words/CURRENT = this spec's args only)
```

**Option specs:**
```
-optname               # option, arg in next word
-optname-              # arg must be in same word (-xARG)
-optname+              # arg in same word or next (-xARG or -x ARG)
-optname=              # arg after = or next word (-x=ARG or -x ARG)
-optname=-             # arg must follow = (-x=ARG only)
*-optname              # option may repeat
-+optname / +-optname  # both -opt and +opt valid
-optname[explanation]  # with description
```

**Option arguments:**
```
:message:action        # required option argument
::message:action       # optional option argument
:*pattern:msg:action   # multiple args until pattern
:*:message:action      # all remaining args for this option
```

**Exclusion lists:**
```zsh
'(-b -c)-a[desc]'                          # -a excludes -b, -c
'(-v --verbose)'{-v,--verbose}'[verbose]'  # short/long mutual
'(- :)'{-h,--help}'[help]'                # - = no more opts, : = no args
'(*)-a[desc]'                             # -a excludes rest-args
'!-old[deprecated]'                       # parsed but not completed
```

**Option groups (mutually exclusive):**
```zsh
_arguments \
  + '(operation)' \
    {-c,--compress}'[compress]' \
    {-d,--decompress}'[decompress]'
# Access: $opt_args[operation--c]
```

**Argument sets (mutually exclusive command modes):**
```zsh
_arguments \
  -a \
  - set1 -c ':arg:(x y)' \
  - set2 -d ':arg:(a b)'
```

### Actions

| Action | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `(item1 item2)` | Complete from literal list |
| `((item1\:desc1 item2\:desc2))` | List with descriptions |
| `->state` | Set `$state`, return to caller |
| `{eval-string}` | Eval shell code |
| `= action` | Insert dummy word, then do action |
| ` func args...` | Call function (leading space = no auto compadd opts) |
| `func args...` | Call function (compadd opts auto-inserted) |
| ` ` (space) | Display message only |

### State machine variables

When using `->state`, these are set by `_arguments`:

```zsh
local context state state_descr line
typeset -A opt_args

_arguments -C \
  '...' \
  '1:cmd:->commands' \
  '*::arg:->args' && ret=0

# $state       - array of active states
# $state_descr - corresponding messages
# $line        - normal arguments (non-option words)
# $opt_args    - associative array: option -> value
# $context     - context string for each state
```

### Deriving specs from --help

```zsh
_arguments -- \
  '*=FILE*:file:_files' \
  '*=DIR*:directory:_files -/' \
  '*\*:toggle:(yes no)'
```

The `--` form runs the command with `--help` and infers specs.
The helpspecs after `--` provide type mappings for argument placeholders.

Additional `--` form options:
- `-l`: Use long option format
- `-i pats`: Ignore options matching patterns
- `-s pair`: Option/arg separator (e.g., `-s =`)

## _describe

Complete items with descriptions.

```zsh
_describe [-12VJ] [-t tag] description specs... [-- specs...]
```

```zsh
local -a items=(
  'opt1:description one'
  'opt2:description two'
)
_describe 'thing' items

# Multiple arrays with different tags
local -a cmds=('start:start it' 'stop:stop it')
local -a opts=('--force:force it')
_describe 'command' cmds -- opts

# With tag for zstyle control
_describe -t commands 'command' cmds
```

## _alternative

Offer completions from multiple sources with different tags.

```zsh
_alternative [-O name] [-C name] spec...
```

Each spec: `tag:description:action`

```zsh
_alternative \
  'users:user:_users' \
  'hosts:host:_hosts' \
  'files:file:_files'

# With literal list
_alternative \
  'actions:action:(start stop restart)' \
  'services:service:_systemd_units'
```

## _values

Complete keyword/value pairs, optionally separated.

```zsh
_values [-s sep] [-S vsep] [-w] name desc...
```

```zsh
# Comma-separated feature flags
_values -s , 'feature' \
  'debug[enable debug]' \
  'verbose[verbose output]' \
  'color[colorize]::when:(auto always never)' \
  'log[log file]:file:_files'

# With = separator for key=value
_values -s , -S = 'settings' \
  'timeout:seconds:' \
  'retries:count:'
```

Value specs follow similar conventions to `_arguments`:
- `name[description]` — simple flag
- `name[description]:message:action` — with required argument
- `name[description]::message:action` — with optional argument

## _files / _path_files

Complete filenames. Prefer `_files` over `_path_files`.

```zsh
_files [-/] [-f] [-g pattern] [-W paths] [-F ignored]
```

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `-f` | All files (default) |
| `-/` | Directories only |
| `-g pattern` | Files matching glob |
| `-W paths` | Prepend paths for testing (not inserted) |
| `-F ignored` | Patterns to ignore |

```zsh
_files                        # all files
_files -/                     # directories only
_files -g '*.{go,mod}'        # Go files
_files -W /usr/share/doc      # relative to dir
_files -F '(*.o *.a)'         # ignore object files
```

Also accepts compadd options: `-J`, `-V`, `-1`, `-2`, `-n`, `-X`, `-M`, `-P`, `-S`, `-q`, `-r`, `-R`.

## _pick_variant

Detect command variants (GNU vs BSD, etc.).

```zsh
_pick_variant [-b builtin-label] [-c command] [-r name] \
  label=pattern ... default-label [arg ...]
```

```zsh
local variant
_pick_variant -r variant gnu='(GNU|Free)' bsd unix --version
case $variant in
  gnu)  # GNU-specific options
    args+=('--color[colorize]::when:(auto always never)') ;;
  bsd)  # BSD-specific options
    args+=('-G[colorize]') ;;
esac
```

Results are cached in `$_cmd_variant`. The command can be overridden with `-c`.
Use `-b label` to detect shell builtins.

## _sequence

Complete separated lists using another function for each item.

```zsh
_sequence [-s sep] [-n max] [-d] function [-- args...]
```

```zsh
_sequence -s : _files -/          # PATH-like: colon-separated dirs
_sequence -s , _users              # comma-separated users
_sequence -s , -n 3 _hosts         # max 3 comma-separated hosts
```

`-d` allows duplicate values. Default separator is `,`.

## _multi_parts

Complete path-like strings segment by segment.

```zsh
_multi_parts [-i] sep array
```

```zsh
local -a pkgs=(com.example.foo com.example.bar org.apache.commons)
_multi_parts . pkgs    # completes: com. -> com.example. -> com.example.foo

local -a paths=(/usr/local/bin /usr/local/lib /usr/share)
_multi_parts / paths   # completes each path segment
```

`-i` inserts unique match even if it requires multiple separators.

## _sep_parts

Complete multi-segment strings with separators between arrays.

```zsh
_sep_parts array1 sep1 array2 [sep2 array3 ...]
```

```zsh
_sep_parts '(alice bob)' @ '(example.com gmail.com)'
# Completes: alice@example.com, bob@gmail.com, etc.

local -a users=(root admin) hosts=(web1 web2 db1)
_sep_parts users @ hosts
```

## _guard

Conditionally display completions based on pattern matching.

```zsh
_guard pattern description
```

```zsh
# Only show "numeric value" hint when input looks numeric
_arguments '-n-: :_guard "[0-9]#" "numeric value"'

# Guard for hex values
_guard '[0-9a-fA-F]#' 'hex value'
```

## _message

Display a help message without generating matches.

```zsh
_message [-r] description
```

```zsh
_message 'enter a URL'
_message -r 'pre-formatted message'  # no style lookup
```

Uses the `format` style with `messages` tag.

## _call_program

Run an external command. The command is user-overridable via `zstyle`.

```zsh
_call_program tag command args...
```

```zsh
local -a targets
targets=( ${(f)"$(_call_program targets make -qp 2>/dev/null)"} )
compadd -a targets
```

Users can override via: `zstyle ':completion:*:targets' command 'my-command'`

## _gnu_generic

Quick completion from `--help` output. Register with `compdef`.

```zsh
compdef _gnu_generic mycommand
```

## _regex_arguments / _regex_words

For complex grammars that don't fit `_arguments`.

```zsh
local -a reply
_regex_words mydb-commands 'mydb commands' \
  'add:add an entry:$mydb_add_cmds' \
  'show:show entries'
_regex_arguments _mydb "$reply[@]"
_mydb "$@"
```

## _numbers

Complete numbers with unit suffixes.

```zsh
_numbers [-t tag] [-u units] [-l min] [-m max] [-d default] [-N] [-f] \
  description [suffix...]
```

```zsh
_numbers -u seconds age :s:seconds m:minutes h:hours d:days
_numbers -l 1 -m 100 -f 'percentage'
```

Prefix suffix with `:` for default unit.

## Caching Functions

```zsh
local cache_id="mycmd_completions"
if _cache_invalid $cache_id || ! _retrieve_cache $cache_id; then
  # expensive computation
  local -a completions=( $(mycmd list --all 2>/dev/null) )
  _store_cache $cache_id completions
fi
compadd -a completions
```

- `_store_cache id params...` — save named parameters to cache file
- `_retrieve_cache id` — restore parameters from cache (returns 0 on success)
- `_cache_invalid id` — check if cache needs rebuild (returns 0 if invalid)

Cache directory controlled by `cache-path` style (default: `~/.zcompcache`).
Caching only active when `use-cache` style is set.

## Tag System

Tags classify completion matches. Users configure via `tag-order` style.

```zsh
local expl ret=1

# Register tags
_tags files directories users

# Loop over tag groups
while _tags; do
  _requested files expl 'file' _files && ret=0
  _requested directories expl 'directory' _files -/ && ret=0
  _requested users expl 'user' _users && ret=0
  (( ret )) || break
done
return ret
```

### Key tag functions

- `_tags tag...` — register available tags, start iteration
- `_requested tag [name descr [cmd args...]]` — check if tag is wanted
- `_next_label tag name descr` — iterate over tag labels
- `_all_labels tag name descr [cmd args...]` — shorthand for _next_label loop
- `_description [-x] tag name descr` — set up description for tag

## _all_labels

Convenient wrapper for the `_next_label` loop.

```zsh
local expl
if _requested foo; then
  local -a matches=(one two three)
  _all_labels foo expl 'items' compadd -- $matches
fi
```

## _requested

Test if a tag has been requested and optionally complete in one call.

```zsh
# Simple test
if _requested files; then
  _files && ret=0
fi

# Test + complete in one call
_requested files expl 'file' _files && ret=0
```

## _normal

Standard function for completing in normal command contexts.
Used internally but useful when re-dispatching after modifying `$words`.

```zsh
# Strip precommand and re-dispatch
words=(${words[2,-1]})
(( CURRENT-- ))
_normal -p $service
```

```zsh
local expl ret=1

# Register tags
_tags files directories users

# Loop over tag groups
while _tags; do
  _requested files expl 'file' _files && ret=0
  _requested directories expl 'directory' _files -/ && ret=0
  _requested users expl 'user' _users && ret=0
  (( ret )) || break
done
return ret
```

### Key tag functions

- `_tags tag...` — register available tags, start iteration
- `_requested tag [name descr [cmd args...]]` — check if tag is wanted
- `_next_label tag name descr` — iterate over tag labels
- `_all_labels tag name descr [cmd args...]` — shorthand for _next_label loop
- `_description [-x] tag name descr` — set up description for tag

## _all_labels

Convenient wrapper for the `_next_label` loop.

```zsh
local expl
if _requested foo; then
  local -a matches=(one two three)
  _all_labels foo expl 'items' compadd -- $matches
fi
```

## _requested

Test if a tag has been requested and optionally complete in one call.

```zsh
# Simple test
if _requested files; then
  _files && ret=0
fi

# Test + complete in one call
_requested files expl 'file' _files && ret=0
```

## _normal

Standard function for completing in normal command contexts.
Used internally but useful when re-dispatching after modifying `$words`.

```zsh
# Strip precommand and re-dispatch
words=(${words[2,-1]})
(( CURRENT-- ))
_normal -p $service
```

content.text: Now let me create the test cases. I'll design 3 realistic prompts that test different complexity levels — a simple command, a subcommand-based CLI, and a complex tool with dynamic completions.

input.content:
{
  "skill_name": "zsh-completion",
  "evals": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "prompt": "Write a Zsh completion script for the `jq` command. It should complete common options like --raw-output/-r, --compact-output/-c, --slurp/-s, --null-input/-n, --exit-status/-e, --sort-keys/-S, --tab, --indent, --arg, --argjson, --slurpfile, --jsonargs, --rawfile, --from-file/-f, --join-output/-j. The first positional argument should show a message 'jq filter expression'. The second positional argument and beyond should complete files with a .json glob.",
      "expected_output": "A complete _jq completion script using _arguments with proper brace expansion for short/long pairs, correct exclusion lists, file completion with -g '*.json' for input files, and proper #compdef header.",
      "files": [],
      "expectations": [
        "The output starts with #compdef jq",
        "Uses _arguments (not compctl or compadd directly for the main structure)",
        "Uses brace expansion for short/long option pairs like '{-r,--raw-output}'",
        "Includes exclusion lists in parentheses for mutually exclusive options",
        "Completes .json files for input file arguments using _files -g",
        "Has a proper function wrapper _jq() { ... }; _jq \"$@\"",
        "The --arg option takes two arguments (name and value)",
        "The --from-file/-f option completes files",
        "Declares local curcontext, state, line, ret and typeset -A opt_args if using ->state"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "prompt": "Write a Zsh completion for `gh` (GitHub CLI). Focus on the top-level subcommands: auth, browse, codespace, gist, issue, pr, release, repo, run, workflow, alias, api, completion, config, extension, label, ruleset, search, secret, ssh-key, status, variable, gpg-key, cache. For the `pr` subcommand, implement completions for its sub-subcommands: create, list, merge, view, checkout, close, comment, diff, edit, ready, reopen, review, status, checks, lock, unlock. The `pr view` subcommand should take a PR number and complete options --web/-w, --json, --jq, --comments. The `pr create` subcommand should complete options --title/-t, --body/-b, --base/-B, --head/-H, --draft/-d, --fill/-f, --web/-w, --assignee/-a, --reviewer/-r, --label/-l, --project/-p, --milestone/-m.",
      "expected_output": "A multi-level completion script with top-level subcommand dispatch, nested pr subcommand dispatch, and per-subcommand option completion. Should use _arguments -C with ->state for subcommand routing.",
      "files": [],
      "expectations": [
        "The output starts with #compdef gh",
        "Uses _arguments -C for subcommand dispatch with ->state",
        "Has a case statement dispatching to all top-level subcommands",
        "The pr subcommand has its own nested _arguments -C with sub-subcommands",
        "pr create has proper short/long option pairs with brace expansion",
        "pr view includes --web/-w and --json options",
        "Uses _describe for listing subcommands with descriptions",
        "Properly updates curcontext for nested subcommands",
        "Has separate helper functions for major subcommands (not everything inline)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": 3,
      "prompt": "Write a Zsh completion for a CLI tool called `kubectl-argo-rollouts` (the Argo Rollouts kubectl plugin). It has subcommands: get, set, create, promote, retry, abort, status, dashboard, lint, undo, list, pause, unpause, notifications, version. Global options are: --loglevel (choices: debug, info, warn, error), --namespace/-n (should complete Kubernetes namespaces dynamically by running 'kubectl get namespaces -o name'), --kubeconfig (file completion). The 'get' subcommand takes a subcommand (rollout or experiment), then a name which should be completed dynamically by running 'kubectl-argo-rollouts list rollouts -n $namespace 2>/dev/null'. The 'promote' subcommand takes a rollout name (same dynamic completion) and has --full flag. Use _call_program for dynamic completions and implement caching for namespace completion.",
      "expected_output": "A completion script with dynamic completions via _call_program, proper caching with _store_cache/_retrieve_cache, namespace completion from kubectl, and nested subcommand dispatch.",
      "files": [],
      "expectations": [
        "The output starts with #compdef kubectl-argo-rollouts",
        "Uses _call_program for dynamic completions (not bare command substitution)",
        "Implements caching using _cache_invalid, _retrieve_cache, _store_cache",
        "Completes namespaces dynamically from kubectl output",
        "The get subcommand has nested routing for rollout/experiment",
        "The promote subcommand completes rollout names dynamically",
        "Global options use proper brace expansion for -n/--namespace",
        "--loglevel uses a choice list: (debug info warn error)",
        "--kubeconfig uses _files for file completion"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- use-modern-go: Apply modern Go syntax guidelines based on project's Go version. Use when user ask for modern Go code guidelines.
- zsh-completion: Write Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework. Use this skill whenever asked to create, write, fix, debug, or improve a Zsh completion function (_arguments, compdef, #compdef, tab completion). Also trigger when seeing zsh autocompletion, shell completion scripts, _compdef tags, or requests involving command-line tab completion for any CLI tool. Even if the user just says "add completions for X" or "make tab completion work", this skill applies.
- execplan: Create and execute structured ExecPlans — self-contained design documents that guide coding agents through complex implementations. Use this skill whenever the user's task involves multi-file changes, significant refactors, new features with multiple components, architectural changes, or any work that would benefit from a structured plan-then-execute approach. Also trigger when the user explicitly asks for an "execplan", "execution plan", or "structured plan". Even if the user doesn't mention planning, use this skill when the task is clearly too complex for ad-hoc implementation — e.g., tasks touching 3+ files, requiring research before coding, involving migrations, or needing coordinated changes across multiple packages or services. When in doubt about whether a task needs an ExecPlan, it probably does.
- zsh-completion: Write Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework. Use this skill whenever asked to create, write, fix, debug, or improve a Zsh completion function (_arguments, compdef, #compdef, tab completion). Also trigger when seeing zsh autocompletion, shell completion scripts, or _compdef tags.

- oh-my-claudecode:deepinit: Deep codebase initialization with hierarchical AGENTS.md documentation
- oh-my-claudecode:hud: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements)
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-interview: Socratic deep interview with mathematical ambiguity gating before autonomous execution
- oh-my-claudecode:autopilot: Full autonomous execution from idea to working code
- oh-my-claudecode:mcp-setup: Configure popular MCP servers for enhanced agent capabilities
- oh-my-claudecode:verify: Verify that a change really works before you claim completion
- oh-my-claudecode:debug: Diagnose the current OMC session or repo state using logs, traces, state, and focused reproduction
- oh-my-claudecode:ask: Process-first advisor routing for Claude, Codex, or Gemini via `omc ask`, with artifact capture and no raw CLI assembly
- oh-my-claudecode:remember: Review reusable project knowledge and decide what belongs in project memory, notepad, or durable docs
- oh-my-claudecode:skill: Manage local skills - list, add, remove, search, edit, setup wizard
- oh-my-claudecode:ralplan: Consensus planning entrypoint that auto-gates vague ralph/autopilot/team requests before execution
- oh-my-claudecode:configure-notifications: Configure notification integrations (Telegram, Discord, Slack) via natural language
- oh-my-claudecode:visual-verdict: Structured visual QA verdict for screenshot-to-reference comparisons
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-reference: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills.
- oh-my-claudecode:ccg: Claude-Codex-Gemini tri-model orchestration via /ask codex + /ask gemini, then Claude synthesizes results
- oh-my-claudecode:sciomc: Orchestrate parallel scientist agents for comprehensive analysis with AUTO mode
- oh-my-claudecode:trace: Evidence-driven tracing lane that orchestrates competing tracer hypotheses in Claude built-in team mode
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-dive: 2-stage pipeline: trace (causal investigation) -> deep-interview (requirements crystallization) with 3-point injection
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- init: Initialize new CLAUDE.md file(s) and optional skills/hooks with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

The 'promote' subcommand takes a rollout name (same dynamic completion) and has --full flag. Use _call_program for dynamic completions and implement caching for namespace completion.

**File**: `/Users/zchee/.claude/skills/zsh-completion/zsh-completion-workspace/iteration-1/eval-1-jq/with_skill/outputs/_jq`

**Structure**: Uses a flat `_arguments -S -s` call wrapped in the standard `_jq() { ... }; _jq "$@"` pattern, with `local curcontext state line ret=1` and `typeset -A opt_args` declared for proper scoping.

**Completed options**:
- Short/long pairs via brace expansion: `-r`/`--raw-output`, `-c`/`--compact-output`, `-s`/`--slurp`, `-n`/`--null-input`, `-e`/`--exit-status`, `-S`/`--sort-keys`, `-j`/`--join-output`, `-f`/`--from-file`, `-R`/`--raw-input`, `-a`/`--ascii-output`, `-C`/`--color-output`, `-M`/`--monochrome-output`, `-h`/`--help`, `-V`/`--version`
- Long-only options: `--tab`, `--indent` (with `(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)` choices), `--raw-output0`, `--stream`, `--stream-errors`, `--seq`, `--unbuffered`, `--build-configuration`
- Repeatable options with argument pairs: `*--arg`, `*--argjson`, `*--slurpfile`, `*--rawfile`
- Mode switches: `--args`, `--jsonargs`
- `-L` with inline-or-next-word argument (`-L+`) completing directories

**Positional arguments**:
- First positional uses `_guard '|[^-]'` to display "jq filter expression" as a message while avoiding completing when the word starts with `-`
- Remaining positional arguments complete files matching `*.json`

**Mutual exclusions**: Options that conflict with each other (e.g., `--slurp` vs `--null-input`, `--tab` vs `--indent`, `-r`/`-j`/`--raw-output0`) are properly excluded. Help/version/build-configuration use `(- 1 *)` to suppress all other completions, gated by `$ign` so they only appear when no other arguments have been given.</result>

**Key design decisions:**

- **Structure**: `_arguments -C -S` with a state machine for subcommand dispatch, following the standard compsys pattern from the skill reference.
- **Global options**: `--loglevel` with static choices `(debug info warn error)`, `--namespace`/`-n` with brace expansion for mutual exclusion and dynamic namespace completion, `--kubeconfig` with `_files`.
- **`get` subcommand**: Implemented as a nested state machine with `->resource_types` and `->resource_name` states. Completes `rollout` or `experiment` as the resource type, then dynamically completes names via `_call_program`.
- **`promote` subcommand**: Separate helper function with `--full` flag and dynamic rollout name completion.
- **Namespace caching**: Uses `_cache_invalid` / `_retrieve_cache` / `_store_cache` with cache id `kubectl_argo_rollouts_namespaces`. The `namespace/` prefix from `kubectl get namespaces -o name` output is stripped via `${...#namespace/}`.
- **Dynamic rollout names**: `_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names` reads the `--namespace`/`-n` value from `$opt_args` and passes it to `kubectl-argo-rollouts list rollouts -n $namespace 2>/dev/null` via `_call_program`.
- **All other subcommands** (set, create, retry, abort, status, dashboard, lint, undo, list, pause, unpause, notifications, version) have appropriate argument specs with relevant options.</result>

#compdef jq

_jq() {
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1
  typeset -A opt_args

  local ign
  (( $#words > 2 )) && ign='!'

  _arguments -S -s \
    "${ign}(- 1 *)"{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
    "${ign}(- 1 *)"{-V,--version}'[show version]' \
    "${ign}(- 1 *)--build-configuration[show jq's build configuration]" \
    '(-r --raw-output -j --join-output --raw-output0)'{-r,--raw-output}'[output strings without escapes and quotes]' \
    '(-r --raw-output -j --join-output)--raw-output0[implies -r and output NUL after each output]' \
    '(-c --compact-output)'{-c,--compact-output}'[compact instead of pretty-printed output]' \
    '(-s --slurp -n --null-input)'{-s,--slurp}'[read all inputs into an array and use it as the single input value]' \
    '(-n --null-input -s --slurp -R --raw-input --stream --stream-errors)'{-n,--null-input}'[use null as the single input value]' \
    '(-e --exit-status)'{-e,--exit-status}'[set exit status code based on the output]' \
    '(-S --sort-keys)'{-S,--sort-keys}'[sort keys of each object on output]' \
    '(--indent)--tab[use tabs for indentation]' \
    '(--tab)--indent[use n spaces for indentation (max 7)]:spaces:(0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7)' \
    '(-R --raw-input -n --null-input)'{-R,--raw-input}'[read each line as string instead of JSON]' \
    '(-a --ascii-output)'{-a,--ascii-output}'[output strings by only ASCII characters using escape sequences]' \
    '(-C --color-output -M --monochrome-output)'{-C,--color-output}'[colorize JSON output]' \
    '(-C --color-output -M --monochrome-output)'{-M,--monochrome-output}'[disable colored output]' \
    '(-r --raw-output --raw-output0 -j --join-output)'{-j,--join-output}'[implies -r and output without newline after each output]' \
    '(-f --from-file)'{-f,--from-file}'[load the filter from a file]:filter file:_files' \
    '-L+[search modules from the directory]:directory:_files -/' \
    '(--stream-errors -n --null-input)--stream[parse the input value in streaming fashion]' \
    '(--stream -n --null-input)--stream-errors[implies --stream and report parse error as an array]' \
    '--seq[parse input/output as application/json-seq]' \
    '--unbuffered[flush output stream after each output]' \
    '*--arg[set variable to a string value]:variable name: :value:' \
    '*--argjson[set variable to a JSON value]:variable name: :value:' \
    '*--slurpfile[set variable to an array of JSON values read from a file]:variable name: :file:_files' \
    '*--rawfile[set variable to string contents of a file]:variable name: :file:_files' \
    '--args[consume remaining arguments as positional string values]' \
    '--jsonargs[consume remaining arguments as positional JSON values]' \
    '1: :_guard "|[^-]" "jq filter expression"' \
    '*:input file:_files -g "*.json"' && ret=0

  return ret
}

_jq "$@"

5	_gh() {
6	  local -a commands

8

11	    '--version[Show gh version]' \
12	    '1: :->command' \
13	    '*:: :->args' && ret=0
14	
15	  case $state in
16	    command)
17	      commands=(
18	        'auth:Authenticate gh and git with GitHub'
19	        'browse:Open the repository in the browser'
20	        'codespace:Connect to and manage codespaces'
21	        'gist:Manage gists'
22	        'issue:Manage issues'
23	        'pr:Manage pull requests'
24	        'release:Manage releases'
25	        'repo:Manage repositories'
26	        'run:View details about workflow runs'
27	        'workflow:View details about GitHub Actions workflows'
28	        'alias:Create command shortcuts'
29	        'api:Make an authenticated GitHub API request'
30	        'completion:Generate shell completion scripts'
31	        'config:Manage configuration for gh'
32	        'extension:Manage gh extensions'
33	        'label:Manage labels'
34	        'ruleset:View info about repo rulesets'
35	        'search:Search for repos, issues, and PRs'
36	        'secret:Manage GitHub secrets'
37	        'ssh-key:Manage SSH keys'
38	        'status:Print information about relevant issues, PRs, and notifications'
39	        'variable:Manage GitHub Actions variables'
40	        'gpg-key:Manage GPG keys'
41	        'cache:Manage GitHub Actions caches'
42	      )
43	      _describe -t commands 'gh command' commands && ret=0
44	      ;;
45	    args)
46	      case $words[1] in
47	        pr)
48	          _gh_pr && ret=0
49	          ;;
50	        auth)
51	          _gh_auth && ret=0
52	          ;;

55	          ;;

58	          ;;
59	        *)
60	          _default && ret=0
61	          ;;
62	      esac
63	      ;;
64	  esac
65	
66	  return ret
67	}
