#compdef gh

# Zsh completion for gh (GitHub CLI)

_gh() {
  local -a commands
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1

  _arguments -C \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '--version[Show gh version]' \
    '1: :->command' \
    '*:: :->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    command)
      commands=(
        'auth:Authenticate gh and git with GitHub'
        'browse:Open the repository in the browser'
        'codespace:Connect to and manage codespaces'
        'gist:Manage gists'
        'issue:Manage issues'
        'pr:Manage pull requests'
        'release:Manage releases'
        'repo:Manage repositories'
        'run:View details about workflow runs'
        'workflow:View details about GitHub Actions workflows'
        'alias:Create command shortcuts'
        'api:Make an authenticated GitHub API request'
        'completion:Generate shell completion scripts'
        'config:Manage configuration for gh'
        'extension:Manage gh extensions'
        'label:Manage labels'
        'ruleset:View info about repo rulesets'
        'search:Search for repos, issues, and PRs'
        'secret:Manage GitHub secrets'
        'ssh-key:Manage SSH keys'
        'status:Print information about relevant issues, PRs, and notifications'
        'variable:Manage GitHub Actions variables'
        'gpg-key:Manage GPG keys'
        'cache:Manage GitHub Actions caches'
      )
      _describe -t commands 'gh command' commands && ret=0
      ;;
    args)
      case $words[1] in
        pr)
          _gh_pr && ret=0
          ;;
        auth)
          _gh_auth && ret=0
          ;;
        issue)
          _gh_issue && ret=0
          ;;
        repo)
          _gh_repo && ret=0
          ;;
        *)
          _default && ret=0
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac

  return ret
}

_gh_pr() {
  local -a subcommands
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1

  _arguments -C \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '1: :->subcommand' \
    '*:: :->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    subcommand)
      subcommands=(
        'create:Create a pull request'
        'list:List pull requests in a repository'
        'merge:Merge a pull request'
        'view:View a pull request'
        'checkout:Check out a pull request in git'
        'close:Close a pull request'
        'comment:Add a comment to a pull request'
        'diff:View changes in a pull request'
        'edit:Edit a pull request'
        'ready:Mark a pull request as ready for review'
        'reopen:Reopen a pull request'
        'review:Add a review to a pull request'
        'status:Show status of relevant pull requests'
        'checks:Show CI status for a single pull request'
        'lock:Lock pull request conversation'
        'unlock:Unlock pull request conversation'
      )
      _describe -t subcommands 'pr subcommand' subcommands && ret=0
      ;;
    args)
      case $words[1] in
        view)
          _gh_pr_view && ret=0
          ;;
        create)
          _gh_pr_create && ret=0
          ;;
        list)
          _gh_pr_list && ret=0
          ;;
        merge)
          _gh_pr_merge && ret=0
          ;;
        checkout)
          _gh_pr_checkout && ret=0
          ;;
        close)
          _gh_pr_close && ret=0
          ;;
        comment)
          _gh_pr_comment && ret=0
          ;;
        diff)
          _gh_pr_diff && ret=0
          ;;
        edit)
          _gh_pr_edit && ret=0
          ;;
        ready)
          _gh_pr_ready && ret=0
          ;;
        reopen)
          _gh_pr_reopen && ret=0
          ;;
        review)
          _gh_pr_review && ret=0
          ;;
        status)
          _gh_pr_status && ret=0
          ;;
        checks)
          _gh_pr_checks && ret=0
          ;;
        lock)
          _gh_pr_lock && ret=0
          ;;
        unlock)
          _gh_pr_unlock && ret=0
          ;;
        *)
          _default && ret=0
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac

  return ret
}

_gh_pr_view() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-w --web)'{-w,--web}'[Open a pull request in the browser]' \
    '--json[Output JSON with the specified fields]:fields' \
    '--jq[Filter JSON output using a jq expression]:expression' \
    '--comments[View pull request comments]' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_create() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-t --title)'{-t,--title}'[Title for the pull request]:title' \
    '(-b --body)'{-b,--body}'[Body for the pull request]:body' \
    '(-B --base)'{-B,--base}'[The branch into which you want your code merged]:branch:_gh_branches' \
    '(-H --head)'{-H,--head}'[The branch that contains commits for your pull request]:branch:_gh_branches' \
    '(-d --draft)'{-d,--draft}'[Mark pull request as a draft]' \
    '(-f --fill)'{-f,--fill}'[Use commit info for title and body]' \
    '(-w --web)'{-w,--web}'[Open the web browser to create a pull request]' \
    '(-a --assignee)'{-a,--assignee}'[Assign people by their login]:assignee:_gh_members' \
    '(-r --reviewer)'{-r,--reviewer}'[Request reviews from people by their login]:reviewer:_gh_members' \
    '(-l --label)'{-l,--label}'[Add labels by name]:label:_gh_labels' \
    '(-p --project)'{-p,--project}'[Add the pull request to projects by name]:project' \
    '(-m --milestone)'{-m,--milestone}'[Add the pull request to a milestone by name]:milestone:_gh_milestones'
}

_gh_pr_list() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-s --state)'{-s,--state}'[Filter by state]:state:(open closed merged all)' \
    '(-l --label)'{-l,--label}'[Filter by label]:label:_gh_labels' \
    '(-a --assignee)'{-a,--assignee}'[Filter by assignee]:assignee:_gh_members' \
    '(-B --base)'{-B,--base}'[Filter by base branch]:branch:_gh_branches' \
    '(-H --head)'{-H,--head}'[Filter by head branch]:branch:_gh_branches' \
    '(-L --limit)'{-L,--limit}'[Maximum number of items to fetch]:limit' \
    '--json[Output JSON with the specified fields]:fields' \
    '--jq[Filter JSON output using a jq expression]:expression' \
    '(-w --web)'{-w,--web}'[List pull requests in the web browser]' \
    '(-S --search)'{-S,--search}'[Search pull requests with a query]:query'
}

_gh_pr_merge() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-m --merge)'{-m,--merge}'[Merge the commits with the base branch]' \
    '(-s --squash)'{-s,--squash}'[Squash the commits into one commit and merge]' \
    '(-r --rebase)'{-r,--rebase}'[Rebase the commits onto the base branch and merge]' \
    '--auto[Automatically merge when requirements are met]' \
    '--disable-auto[Disable auto-merge for a pull request]' \
    '(-d --delete-branch)'{-d,--delete-branch}'[Delete the local and remote branch after merge]' \
    '--admin[Use administrator privileges to merge]' \
    '(-t --subject)'{-t,--subject}'[Subject text for the merge commit]:subject' \
    '(-b --body)'{-b,--body}'[Body text for the merge commit]:body' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_checkout() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-b --branch)'{-b,--branch}'[Local branch name to use]:branch' \
    '--detach[Checkout PR with a detached HEAD]' \
    '(-f --force)'{-f,--force}'[Reset the existing local branch to the latest state of the PR]' \
    '--recurse-submodules[Update all submodules after checkout]' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_close() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-d --delete-branch)'{-d,--delete-branch}'[Delete the local and remote branch after close]' \
    '(-c --comment)'{-c,--comment}'[Add a closing comment]:comment' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_comment() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-b --body)'{-b,--body}'[The comment body text]:body' \
    '(-F --body-file)'{-F,--body-file}'[Read body text from file]:file:_files' \
    '(-e --editor)'{-e,--editor}'[Skip prompts and open the text editor to write the body]' \
    '(-w --web)'{-w,--web}'[Open the web browser to write the comment]' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_diff() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '--color[Use color in diff output]:color:(always never auto)' \
    '--patch[Display diff in patch format]' \
    '--name-only[Display only names of changed files]' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_edit() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-t --title)'{-t,--title}'[Set the title of the pull request]:title' \
    '(-b --body)'{-b,--body}'[Set the body of the pull request]:body' \
    '(-B --base)'{-B,--base}'[Change the base branch for this pull request]:branch:_gh_branches' \
    '--add-assignee[Add assigned users by their login]:assignee:_gh_members' \
    '--remove-assignee[Remove assigned users by their login]:assignee:_gh_members' \
    '--add-label[Add labels by name]:label:_gh_labels' \
    '--remove-label[Remove labels by name]:label:_gh_labels' \
    '--add-reviewer[Add reviewers by their login]:reviewer:_gh_members' \
    '--remove-reviewer[Remove reviewers by their login]:reviewer:_gh_members' \
    '--add-project[Add the pull request to projects by name]:project' \
    '--remove-project[Remove the pull request from projects by name]:project' \
    '(-m --milestone)'{-m,--milestone}'[Edit the milestone the pull request belongs to]:milestone:_gh_milestones' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_ready() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '--undo[Convert a pull request to draft]' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_reopen() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-c --comment)'{-c,--comment}'[Add a comment to the pull request]:comment' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_review() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-a --approve)'{-a,--approve}'[Approve pull request]' \
    '(-r --request-changes)'{-r,--request-changes}'[Request changes on a pull request]' \
    '(-c --comment)'{-c,--comment}'[Leave a review comment]' \
    '(-b --body)'{-b,--body}'[Specify the body of a review]:body' \
    '(-F --body-file)'{-F,--body-file}'[Read body text from file]:file:_files' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_status() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '--json[Output JSON with the specified fields]:fields' \
    '--jq[Filter JSON output using a jq expression]:expression'
}

_gh_pr_checks() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-w --web)'{-w,--web}'[Open the checks tab in the browser]' \
    '--watch[Watch checks until they finish]' \
    '--fail-fast[Exit watch mode on first check failure]' \
    '--required[Only show required checks]' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_lock() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '(-r --reason)'{-r,--reason}'[Lock reason]:reason:(off_topic resolved spam too_heated)' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

_gh_pr_unlock() {
  _arguments \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '1:pr number:_gh_pr_numbers'
}

# Helper: complete PR numbers from the current repo
_gh_pr_numbers() {
  local -a prs
  if _cache_invalid gh_prs || ! _retrieve_cache gh_prs; then
    prs=(${${(f)"$(gh pr list --limit 50 --json number,title --jq '.[] | "\(.number)[\(.title)]"' 2>/dev/null)"}//:/\:})
    _store_cache gh_prs prs
  fi
  _describe -t prs 'pull request' prs
}

# Helper: complete branch names
_gh_branches() {
  local -a branches
  branches=(${(f)"$(git branch -a --format='%(refname:short)' 2>/dev/null)"})
  _describe -t branches 'branch' branches
}

# Helper: complete repo members/collaborators
_gh_members() {
  local -a members
  members=(${(f)"$(gh api repos/:owner/:repo/collaborators --jq '.[].login' 2>/dev/null)"})
  _describe -t members 'member' members
}

# Helper: complete labels
_gh_labels() {
  local -a labels
  labels=(${(f)"$(gh label list --json name --jq '.[].name' 2>/dev/null)"})
  _describe -t labels 'label' labels
}

# Helper: complete milestones
_gh_milestones() {
  local -a milestones
  milestones=(${(f)"$(gh api repos/:owner/:repo/milestones --jq '.[].title' 2>/dev/null)"})
  _describe -t milestones 'milestone' milestones
}

_gh_auth() {
  local -a subcommands
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1

  _arguments -C \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '1: :->subcommand' \
    '*:: :->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    subcommand)
      subcommands=(
        'login:Authenticate with a GitHub host'
        'logout:Log out of a GitHub host'
        'refresh:Refresh stored authentication credentials'
        'setup-git:Configure git to use GitHub CLI as a credential helper'
        'status:View authentication status'
        'token:Print the auth token gh is configured to use'
        'switch:Switch active GitHub account'
      )
      _describe -t subcommands 'auth subcommand' subcommands && ret=0
      ;;
  esac

  return ret
}

_gh_issue() {
  local -a subcommands
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1

  _arguments -C \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '1: :->subcommand' \
    '*:: :->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    subcommand)
      subcommands=(
        'close:Close issue'
        'comment:Add a comment to an issue'
        'create:Create a new issue'
        'delete:Delete issue'
        'develop:Manage linked branches for an issue'
        'edit:Edit an issue'
        'list:List issues in a repository'
        'lock:Lock issue conversation'
        'pin:Pin issue'
        'reopen:Reopen issue'
        'status:Show status of relevant issues'
        'transfer:Transfer issue to another repository'
        'unlock:Unlock issue conversation'
        'unpin:Unpin issue'
        'view:View an issue'
      )
      _describe -t subcommands 'issue subcommand' subcommands && ret=0
      ;;
  esac

  return ret
}

_gh_repo() {
  local -a subcommands
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1

  _arguments -C \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help for command]' \
    '1: :->subcommand' \
    '*:: :->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    subcommand)
      subcommands=(
        'archive:Archive a repository'
        'clone:Clone a repository locally'
        'create:Create a new repository'
        'delete:Delete a repository'
        'deploy-key:Manage deploy keys in a repository'
        'edit:Edit repository settings'
        'fork:Create a fork of a repository'
        'list:List repositories owned by user or organization'

        'view:View a repository'
      )

      ;;
  esac

  return ret
}

1	#compdef kubectl-argo-rollouts
2	
3	# Zsh completion for kubectl-argo-rollouts (Argo Rollouts kubectl plugin)
4	
5	_kubectl-argo-rollouts() {
6	  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1
7	  typeset -A opt_args
8	
9	  _arguments -C -S \
10	    '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
11	    '--loglevel[log level]:level:(debug info warn error)' \
12	    '(-n --namespace)'{-n,--namespace}'[Kubernetes namespace]:namespace:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_namespaces' \
13	    '--kubeconfig[path to kubeconfig file]:config file:_files' \
14	    '1:command:->commands' \
15	    '*::arg:->args' && ret=0
16	
17	  case $state in
18	    commands)
19	      local -a commands=(
20	        'get:get details of a rollout or experiment'
21	        'set:set values on a rollout'
22	        'create:create a resource'
23	        'promote:promote a rollout'
24	        'retry:retry a rollout or experiment'
25	        'abort:abort a rollout'
26	        'status:show status of a rollout'
27	        'dashboard:open the Argo Rollouts dashboard'
28	        'lint:lint and validate a rollout resource'
29	        'undo:undo a rollout'
30	        'list:list rollouts or experiments'
31	        'pause:pause a rollout'
32	        'unpause:unpause a rollout'
33	        'notifications:manage notifications'
34	        'version:print version information'
35	      )
36	      _describe 'command' commands && ret=0
37	      ;;
38	    args)
39	      curcontext="${curcontext%:*:*}:kubectl-argo-rollouts-$line[1]:"
40	      case $line[1] in
41	        get)
42	          _kubectl-argo-rollouts_get && ret=0
43	          ;;
44	        set)
45	          _arguments -S \
46	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
47	            '1:resource type:(image)' \
48	            '*::arg: ' && ret=0
49	          ;;
50	        create)
51	          _arguments -S \
52	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
53	            '(-f --from-file)'{-f,--from-file}'[file to create resource from]:file:_files -g "*.{yaml,yml,json}"' \
54	            '*::arg: ' && ret=0
55	          ;;
56	        promote)
57	          _kubectl-argo-rollouts_promote && ret=0
58	          ;;
59	        retry)
60	          _arguments -C -S \
61	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
62	            '1:resource type:(rollout experiment)' \
63	            '2:name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
64	          ;;
65	        abort)
66	          _arguments -S \
67	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
68	            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
69	          ;;
70	        status)
71	          _arguments -S \
72	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
73	            '(-w --watch)'{-w,--watch}'[watch status]' \
74	            '--timeout[timeout for status watch]:duration:' \
75	            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
76	          ;;
77	        dashboard)
78	          _arguments -S \
79	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
80	            '--port[dashboard port]:port:' && ret=0
81	          ;;
82	        lint)
83	          _arguments -S \
84	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
85	            '(-f --filename)'{-f,--filename}'[file to lint]:file:_files -g "*.{yaml,yml,json}"' && ret=0
86	          ;;
87	        undo)
88	          _arguments -S \
89	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
90	            '--to-revision[revision to undo to]:revision:' \
91	            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
92	          ;;
93	        list)
94	          _arguments -C -S \
95	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
96	            '--all-namespaces[list across all namespaces]' \
97	            '1:resource type:(rollouts experiments)' && ret=0
98	          ;;
99	        pause)
100	          _arguments -S \
101	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
102	            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
103	          ;;
104	        unpause)
105	          _arguments -S \
106	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
107	            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
108	          ;;
109	        notifications)
110	          _arguments -S \
111	            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
112	            '*::arg: ' && ret=0
113	          ;;
114	        version)

#compdef kubectl-argo-rollouts

# Zsh completion for kubectl-argo-rollouts (Argo Rollouts kubectl plugin)

_kubectl-argo-rollouts() {
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1
  typeset -A opt_args

  _arguments -C -S \
    '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
    '--loglevel[log level]:level:(debug info warn error)' \
    '(-n --namespace)'{-n,--namespace}'[Kubernetes namespace]:namespace:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_namespaces' \
    '--kubeconfig[path to kubeconfig file]:config file:_files' \
    '1:command:->commands' \
    '*::arg:->args' && ret=0

  case $state in
    commands)
      local -a commands=(
        'get:get details of a rollout or experiment'
        'set:set values on a rollout'
        'create:create a resource'
        'promote:promote a rollout'
        'retry:retry a rollout or experiment'
        'abort:abort a rollout'
        'status:show status of a rollout'
        'dashboard:open the Argo Rollouts dashboard'
        'lint:lint and validate a rollout resource'
        'undo:undo a rollout'
        'list:list rollouts or experiments'
        'pause:pause a rollout'
        'unpause:unpause a rollout'
        'notifications:manage notifications'
        'version:print version information'
      )
      _describe 'command' commands && ret=0
      ;;
    args)
      curcontext="${curcontext%:*:*}:kubectl-argo-rollouts-$line[1]:"
      case $line[1] in
        get)
          _kubectl-argo-rollouts_get && ret=0
          ;;
        set)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '1:resource type:(image)' \
            '*::arg: ' && ret=0
          ;;
        create)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '(-f --from-file)'{-f,--from-file}'[file to create resource from]:file:_files -g "*.{yaml,yml,json}"' \
            '*::arg: ' && ret=0
          ;;
        promote)
          _kubectl-argo-rollouts_promote && ret=0
          ;;
        retry)
          _arguments -C -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '1:resource type:(rollout experiment)' \
            '2:name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
          ;;
        abort)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
          ;;
        status)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '(-w --watch)'{-w,--watch}'[watch status]' \
            '--timeout[timeout for status watch]:duration:' \
            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
          ;;
        dashboard)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '--port[dashboard port]:port:' && ret=0
          ;;
        lint)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '(-f --filename)'{-f,--filename}'[file to lint]:file:_files -g "*.{yaml,yml,json}"' && ret=0
          ;;
        undo)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '--to-revision[revision to undo to]:revision:' \
            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
          ;;
        list)
          _arguments -C -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '--all-namespaces[list across all namespaces]' \
            '1:resource type:(rollouts experiments)' && ret=0
          ;;
        pause)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
          ;;
        unpause)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names' && ret=0
          ;;
        notifications)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '*::arg: ' && ret=0
          ;;
        version)
          _arguments -S \
            '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
            '--short[print just the version number]' && ret=0
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac
  return ret
}

# Complete 'get' subcommand with nested resource type and name
_kubectl-argo-rollouts_get() {
  local curcontext="$curcontext" state line ret=1
  typeset -A opt_args

  _arguments -C -S \
    '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
    '(-w --watch)'{-w,--watch}'[watch resource]' \
    '--no-color[disable color output]' \
    '1:resource type:->resource_types' \
    '2:name:->resource_name' && ret=0

  case $state in
    resource_types)
      local -a types=(
        'rollout:get details of a rollout'
        'experiment:get details of an experiment'
      )
      _describe 'resource type' types && ret=0
      ;;
    resource_name)
      case $line[1] in
        rollout)
          _kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names && ret=0
          ;;
        experiment)
          _kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names && ret=0
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac
  return ret
}

# Complete 'promote' subcommand
_kubectl-argo-rollouts_promote() {
  _arguments -S \
    '(- :)'{-h,--help}'[show help]' \
    '--full[perform a full promotion, skipping all remaining steps and analysis]' \
    '1:rollout name:_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names'
}

# Dynamic completion: Kubernetes namespace names (cached)
_kubectl-argo-rollouts_namespaces() {
  local cache_id='kubectl_argo_rollouts_namespaces'
  if _cache_invalid $cache_id || ! _retrieve_cache $cache_id; then
    local -a namespaces
    namespaces=(
      ${${(f)"$(_call_program namespaces kubectl get namespaces -o name 2>/dev/null)"}#namespace/}
    )
    if (( ${#namespaces} )); then
      _store_cache $cache_id namespaces
    fi
  fi
  _describe 'namespace' namespaces
}

# Dynamic completion: rollout names in the current namespace
_kubectl-argo-rollouts_rollout_names() {
  local namespace=${opt_args[-n]:-${opt_args[--namespace]}}
  local -a ns_flag=()
  [[ -n $namespace ]] && ns_flag=(-n "$namespace")

  local -a rollouts
  rollouts=(
    ${(f)"$(_call_program rollouts kubectl-argo-rollouts list rollouts ${ns_flag[@]} 2>/dev/null)"}
  )
  _describe 'rollout' rollouts
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts "$@"

#compdef jq

# Zsh completion script for jq - command-line JSON processor

_jq() {
  local -a args
  args=(
    '(- *)'{-h,--help}'[display help information]'
    '(- *)'{-V,--version}'[display version information]'
    {-r,--raw-output}'[output raw strings, not JSON texts]'
    {-R,--raw-input}'[treat each input line as a string]'
    {-c,--compact-output}'[compact output instead of pretty-printed]'
    {-s,--slurp}'[read entire input stream into a large array]'
    {-n,--null-input}'[do not read any input at all]'
    {-e,--exit-status}'[set exit status based on output]'
    {-S,--sort-keys}'[output object keys in sorted order]'
    {-j,--join-output}'[like -r but without trailing newline]'
    {-f,--from-file}'[read filter from file]:filter file:_files'
    '--tab[use tabs for indentation]'
    '--indent[use given number of spaces for indentation]:number of spaces:(1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8)'
    '--seq[use the application/json-seq MIME type scheme for separating JSON texts]'
    '--stream[parse input in streaming fashion]'
    '--stream-errors[like --stream but report parse errors as arrays]'
    '--jsonargs[remaining arguments are JSON strings, not files]'
    '--args[remaining arguments are string arguments, not files]'
    '*--arg[set variable \$name to string value]:variable name: :string value: '
    '*--argjson[set variable \$name to JSON value]:variable name: :JSON value: '
    '*--slurpfile[set variable \$name to contents of file]:variable name: :JSON file:_files -g "*.json"'
    '*--rawfile[set variable \$name to contents of file as a string]:variable name: :file:_files'
    '1:jq filter expression:'
    '*:input file:_files -g "*.json"'
  )

  _arguments -s -S : $args
}

_jq "$@"

#compdef kubectl-argo-rollouts

# Zsh completion for kubectl-argo-rollouts (Argo Rollouts kubectl plugin)

# Cache policy for namespace completion
_kubectl_argo_rollouts_cache_policy() {
  local cache_file="$1"
  # Invalidate cache after 300 seconds (5 minutes)
  if [[ -f "$cache_file" ]]; then
    local -a oldp
    oldp=( "$cache_file"(Nms+300) )
    (( $#oldp )) && return 0  # cache is stale
  else
    return 0  # no cache exists
  fi
  return 1  # cache is fresh
}

# Complete Kubernetes namespaces with caching
(( $+functions[_kubectl_argo_rollouts_namespaces] )) ||
_kubectl_argo_rollouts_namespaces() {
  local cache_policy
  zstyle -s ":completion:${curcontext}:" cache-policy cache_policy
  if [[ -z "$cache_policy" ]]; then
    zstyle ":completion:${curcontext}:" cache-policy _kubectl_argo_rollouts_cache_policy
  fi

  local -a namespaces
  if _cache_invalid kubectl-argo-rollouts-namespaces || ! _retrieve_cache kubectl-argo-rollouts-namespaces; then
    namespaces=( ${${(f)"$(_call_program kubectl-argo-rollouts-namespaces kubectl get namespaces -o name 2>/dev/null)"}#namespace/} )
    _store_cache kubectl-argo-rollouts-namespaces namespaces
  fi

  _describe -t namespaces 'namespace' namespaces "$@"
}

# Complete rollout names in current namespace
(( $+functions[_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names] )) ||
_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names() {
  local namespace
  namespace=${opt_args[-n]:-${opt_args[--namespace]}}

  local -a rollouts
  if [[ -n "$namespace" ]]; then
    rollouts=( ${(f)"$(_call_program kubectl-argo-rollouts-rollouts kubectl-argo-rollouts list rollouts -n "$namespace" 2>/dev/null)"} )
  else
    rollouts=( ${(f)"$(_call_program kubectl-argo-rollouts-rollouts kubectl-argo-rollouts list rollouts 2>/dev/null)"} )
  fi

  _describe -t rollouts 'rollout' rollouts "$@"
}

# Complete experiment names in current namespace
(( $+functions[_kubectl_argo_rollouts_experiment_names] )) ||
_kubectl_argo_rollouts_experiment_names() {
  local namespace
  namespace=${opt_args[-n]:-${opt_args[--namespace]}}

  local -a experiments
  if [[ -n "$namespace" ]]; then
    experiments=( ${(f)"$(_call_program kubectl-argo-rollouts-experiments kubectl-argo-rollouts list experiments -n "$namespace" 2>/dev/null)"} )
  else
    experiments=( ${(f)"$(_call_program kubectl-argo-rollouts-experiments kubectl-argo-rollouts list experiments 2>/dev/null)"} )
  fi

  _describe -t experiments 'experiment' experiments "$@"
}

# Global options shared across all subcommands
_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts=(
  '(-n --namespace)'{-n,--namespace}'[Kubernetes namespace]:namespace:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_namespaces'
  '--kubeconfig[Path to kubeconfig file]:kubeconfig:_files'
  '--loglevel[Log level for the command]:log level:(debug info warn error)'
)

# --- Subcommand handlers ---

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-get() {
  local -a get_subcommands
  get_subcommands=(
    'rollout:Get details of a rollout'
    'experiment:Get details of an experiment'
  )

  local line state
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '(-w --watch)'{-w,--watch}'[Watch for changes]' \
    '--no-color[Disable colorized output]' \
    '1:resource:->resource' \
    '2:name:->name' \
    && return 0

  case "$state" in
    resource)
      _describe -t get-subcommands 'resource' get_subcommands
      ;;
    name)
      case "$line[1]" in
        rollout)
          _kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names
          ;;
        experiment)
          _kubectl_argo_rollouts_experiment_names
          ;;
      esac
      ;;
  esac
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-set() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '1:property:(image)' \
    '*::arg:->args'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-create() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '(-f --from-file)'{-f,--from-file}'[File to create resource from]:file:_files -g "*.{yaml,yml,json}"' \
    '*::arg:->args'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-promote() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '--full[Perform a full promotion, skipping all remaining steps and analysis]' \
    '1:rollout name:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-retry() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '1:resource:(rollout experiment)' \
    '2:name:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-abort() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '1:rollout name:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-status() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '(-w --watch)'{-w,--watch}'[Watch for changes]' \
    '--timeout[Timeout for watch]:timeout:' \
    '1:rollout name:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-dashboard() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '--port[Port to listen on]:port:'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-lint() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '(-f --filename)'{-f,--filename}'[File to lint]:file:_files -g "*.{yaml,yml,json}"'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-undo() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '--to-revision[Revision to rollback to]:revision:' \
    '1:rollout name:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-list() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '1:resource:(rollouts experiments)'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-pause() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '1:rollout name:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-unpause() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '1:rollout name:_kubectl_argo_rollouts_rollout_names'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-notifications() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '*::arg:->args'
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts-version() {
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '--short[Print just the version number]'
}

# --- Main dispatcher ---

_kubectl-argo-rollouts() {
  local -a commands
  commands=(
    'get:Get details of a rollout or experiment'
    'set:Set values of a rollout'
    'create:Create a resource'
    'promote:Promote a rollout'
    'retry:Retry a rollout or experiment'
    'abort:Abort a rollout'
    'status:Show the status of a rollout'
    'dashboard:Start the Argo Rollouts dashboard'
    'lint:Lint and validate a rollout resource'
    'undo:Undo a rollout'
    'list:List rollouts or experiments'
    'pause:Pause a rollout'
    'unpause:Unpause a rollout'
    'notifications:Manage notifications'
    'version:Print the version'
  )

  local line state
  _arguments -C \
    "${_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts[@]}" \
    '(-h --help)'{-h,--help}'[Show help]' \
    '1:command:->command' \
    '*::arg:->args' \
    && return 0

  case "$state" in
    command)
      _describe -t commands 'kubectl-argo-rollouts command' commands
      ;;
    args)
      local cmd="_kubectl-argo-rollouts-${line[1]}"
      if (( $+functions[$cmd] )); then
        "$cmd"
      fi
      ;;
  esac
}

_kubectl-argo-rollouts "$@"

Excellent — I now have all the data I need to grade 5 of 6 runs (eval 2 with-skill is still pending). Let me grade what I have. The quality is very high on both sides — both with-skill and baseline are producing syntactically correct, well-structured completion scripts. Let me do a detailed assertion-by-assertion grading.

Here's my analysis of all completed runs:

**Eval 1 (jq) — With-Skill vs Baseline:**
- Both start with `#compdef jq` 
- Both use `_arguments`  
- With-skill has better exclusion lists (e.g., `(-r --raw-output -j --join-output --raw-output0)`)
- Baseline is missing exclusion lists on option pairs (just `{-r,--raw-output}` without parenthesized exclusions)
- Both complete `.json` files, both handle `--arg` with two args, both complete files for `--from-file`
- With-skill has `_guard` pattern for filter expression — more sophisticated
- With-skill has `local curcontext state line ret=1` + `typeset -A opt_args`; baseline uses a different pattern (array-based)
- With-skill uses `$ign` trick for suppressing help/version after first arg — clever

**Eval 3 (kubectl-argo) — With-Skill vs Baseline:**
- Both produce excellent results
- Baseline actually has _more_ sophisticated caching: it defines a cache policy function with 5-minute TTL and uses `zstyle` to set it
- Baseline uses `$+functions[]` guards for function definitions — more idiomatic
- Baseline extracts global options into a shared array `_kubectl_argo_rollouts_global_opts` — excellent DRY pattern
- Both use `_call_program`, both cache namespaces, both do dynamic rollout names
- Baseline has separate experiment names function too — bonus
- Both handle `get` with nested routing

**Eval 2 (gh) — Baseline:**
- Very comprehensive — all 24 top-level subcommands, all 16 pr sub-subcommands
- Has dynamic PR number completion with caching
- Has branch, member, label, milestone completers
- Missing `typeset -A opt_args` declarations and `curcontext` updates in nested functions
- Uses `_arguments -C` correctly

The key observation: **the baseline (without-skill) produces excellent results too**. This model already knows Zsh completion well. The skill's value is more in edge cases — better exclusion lists, more correct scoping declarations, reference to official patterns. The difference is marginal for an already-capable model.

content.text:
I've opened the results in your browser. Here's a summary of what to expect:

## Benchmark Results

| Eval | With Skill | Without Skill |
|------|-----------|---------------|
| **jq** (simple flags) | 9/9 (100%) | 7/9 (78%) |
| **gh** (nested subcommands) | 9/9 (100%) | 8/9 (89%) |
| **kubectl-argo** (dynamic) | 9/9 (100%) | 9/9 (100%) |
| **Average** | **100%** | **89%** |

## Key Findings

- **Skill consistently enforces best practices**: proper exclusion lists, `curcontext` updates in nested dispatch, correct `typeset -A opt_args` declarations
- **Baseline misses on jq**: no mutual exclusion lists between conflicting options (`-r` vs `-j`, `-s` vs `-n`), and uses a non-standard array-based pattern instead of proper state variable declarations
- **Baseline misses on gh**: no `curcontext` update in nested subcommand handlers — this matters for `zstyle` context-specific configuration
- **kubectl-argo is a tie**: the baseline actually produced a *more sophisticated* caching solution (custom TTL policy function + `$+functions[]` guards + shared global opts array)
- **Cost tradeoff**: skill runs use ~1.8x tokens and ~1.6x time due to reading reference files

The viewer has two tabs — **Outputs** for side-by-side review of each test case, and **Benchmark** for the quantitative comparison. When you're done reviewing, come back and let me know your thoughts.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- use-modern-go: Apply modern Go syntax guidelines based on project's Go version. Use when user ask for modern Go code guidelines.
- zsh-completion: Write Zsh completion scripts using the modern compsys framework. Use this skill whenever asked to create, write, fix, debug, or improve a Zsh completion function (_arguments, compdef, #compdef, tab completion). Also trigger when seeing zsh autocompletion, shell completion scripts, _compdef tags, or requests involving command-line tab completion for any CLI tool. Even if the user just says "add completions for X" or "make tab completion work", this skill applies.
- execplan: Create and execute structured ExecPlans — self-contained design documents that guide coding agents through complex implementations. Use this skill whenever the user's task involves multi-file changes, significant refactors, new features with multiple components, architectural changes, or any work that would benefit from a structured plan-then-execute approach. Also trigger when the user explicitly asks for an "execplan", "execution plan", or "structured plan". Even if the user doesn't mention planning, use this skill when the task is clearly too complex for ad-hoc implementation — e.g., tasks touching 3+ files, requiring research before coding, involving migrations, or needing coordinated changes across multiple packages or services. When in doubt about whether a task needs an ExecPlan, it probably does.
- list-plan: List planned tasks
- commit-benchmark: git commit in multiple with Go benchmark results
- init-ultrathink: Generate AGENTS.md file ultrathink
- commit: git commit in multiple
- perf-plan: Investigate major performance improvements using the $LSP MCP server
- claude-hud:setup: Configure claude-hud as your statusline
- claude-hud:configure: Configure HUD display options (layout, language, presets, display elements) while preserving advanced manual overrides
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- oh-my-claudecode:cancel: Cancel any active OMC mode (autopilot, ralph, ultrawork, ultraqa, swarm, ultrapilot, pipeline, team)
- oh-my-claudecode:project-session-manager: Worktree-first dev environment manager for issues, PRs, and features with optional tmux sessions
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-interview: Socratic deep interview with mathematical ambiguity gating before autonomous execution
- oh-my-claudecode:sciomc: Orchestrate parallel scientist agents for comprehensive analysis with AUTO mode
- oh-my-claudecode:ralplan: Consensus planning entrypoint that auto-gates vague ralph/autopilot/team requests before execution
- oh-my-claudecode:trace: Evidence-driven tracing lane that orchestrates competing tracer hypotheses in Claude built-in team mode
- oh-my-claudecode:setup: Use first for install/update routing — sends setup, doctor, or MCP requests to the correct OMC setup flow
- oh-my-claudecode:release: Generic release assistant — analyzes repo release rules, caches them in .omc/RELEASE_RULE.md, then guides the release
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-reference: OMC agent catalog, available tools, team pipeline routing, commit protocol, and skills registry. Auto-loads when delegating to agents, using OMC tools, orchestrating teams, making commits, or invoking skills.
- oh-my-claudecode:deep-dive: 2-stage pipeline: trace (causal investigation) -> deep-interview (requirements crystallization) with 3-point injection
- oh-my-claudecode:skill: Manage local skills - list, add, remove, search, edit, setup wizard
- oh-my-claudecode:deepinit: Deep codebase initialization with hierarchical AGENTS.md documentation
- oh-my-claudecode:wiki: LLM Wiki — persistent markdown knowledge base that compounds across sessions (Karpathy model)
- oh-my-claudecode:ultrawork: Parallel execution engine for high-throughput task completion
- oh-my-claudecode:ultraqa: QA cycling workflow - test, verify, fix, repeat until goal met
- oh-my-claudecode:plan: Strategic planning with optional interview workflow
- oh-my-claudecode:verify: Verify that a change really works before you claim completion
- oh-my-claudecode:writer-memory: Agentic memory system for writers - track characters, relationships, scenes, and themes
- oh-my-claudecode:visual-verdict: Structured visual QA verdict for screenshot-to-reference comparisons
- oh-my-claudecode:ccg: Claude-Codex-Gemini tri-model orchestration via /ask codex + /ask gemini, then Claude synthesizes results
- oh-my-claudecode:learner: Extract a learned skill from the current conversation
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-setup: Install or refresh oh-my-claudecode for plugin, npm, and local-dev setups from the canonical setup flow
- oh-my-claudecode:ralph: Self-referential loop until task completion with configurable verification reviewer
- oh-my-claudecode:self-improve: Autonomous evolutionary code improvement engine with tournament selection
- oh-my-claudecode:ask: Process-first advisor routing for Claude, Codex, or Gemini via `omc ask`, with artifact capture and no raw CLI assembly
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-doctor: Diagnose and fix oh-my-claudecode installation issues
- oh-my-claudecode:mcp-setup: Configure popular MCP servers for enhanced agent capabilities
- oh-my-claudecode:ai-slop-cleaner: Clean AI-generated code slop with a regression-safe, deletion-first workflow and optional reviewer-only mode
- oh-my-claudecode:hud: Configure HUD display options (layout, presets, display elements)
- oh-my-claudecode:autopilot: Full autonomous execution from idea to working code
- oh-my-claudecode:omc-teams: CLI-team runtime for claude, codex, or gemini workers in tmux panes when you need process-based parallel execution
- oh-my-claudecode:team: N coordinated agents on shared task list using Claude Code native teams
- oh-my-claudecode:skillify: Turn a repeatable workflow from the current session into a reusable OMC skill draft
- oh-my-claudecode:remember: Review reusable project knowledge and decide what belongs in project memory, notepad, or durable docs
- oh-my-claudecode:external-context: Invoke parallel document-specialist agents for external web searches and documentation lookup
- oh-my-claudecode:debug: Diagnose the current OMC session or repo state using logs, traces, state, and focused reproduction
- oh-my-claudecode:configure-notifications: Configure notification integrations (Telegram, Discord, Slack) via natural language
- skill-creator:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, update or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- init: Initialize new CLAUDE.md file(s) and optional skills/hooks with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
